gaps.feature: Filter persistence across navigation (back from order detail)
gaps.rationale: ECL-3.2 calls out 'Filter persists incorrectly' as observed defect. No existing case verifies filter remains applied after navigating to order detail and using browser back.

gaps.feature: Filter + sort combination — filter is preserved when sort is changed
gaps.rationale: ECL-3.2 explicitly names 'Sort order resets on filter' as anti-pattern. ORD-019, ORD-020, ORD-021 each test filter OR sort in isolation, never together.

gaps.feature: Date range — invalid range (start > end) validation
gaps.rationale: ORD-017 only covers valid date range. No negative case for start > end (should validate or auto-swap).

gaps.feature: Date range BVA — same start and end date (single-day boundary)
gaps.rationale: Boundary value: start = end should return orders placed on that exact date (inclusive). Not covered.

gaps.feature: Filter + search combination — both narrow results jointly
gaps.rationale: ORD-015 (search) and ORD-019 (status filter) tested in isolation. No verification that they compose correctly (intersection).

gaps.feature: Pagination + filter combination — filter preserved across page navigation
gaps.rationale: ORD-016 tests pagination on unfiltered list. Filter that yields >1 page of results, then click page 2 — filter must remain applied. Not covered.

gaps.feature: URL query-param deep link / browser refresh restores filter state
gaps.rationale: If filter state is in URL (status, date, page), reload should preserve. If not in URL, the deep link is broken — either behavior should be explicitly verified.

gaps.feature: Search by partial order number (prefix / substring)
gaps.rationale: ORD-015 tests exact order-number match only. Partial / prefix search behavior is undocumented and untested — common buyer use case.

gaps.feature: B2B org-scoped order list — filter applied to org-wide orders, not buyer-only
gaps.rationale: B2B-store buyers (with company role) see their org's orders, not just their own. Filter behavior in this multi-buyer context not covered by existing cases (all assume single USER_DEFAULT scope).

gaps.feature: Active filter indicators — chip-style display of applied filters, individual removal
gaps.rationale: ORD-022 covers global Clear/Reset. Individual filter-chip removal (X on each chip) is a separate interaction not currently covered.

outOfScopeFindings: BL ref hygiene: many existing ORD-* cases reference BL-ORD-003 (partial fulfillment) for filter/search behaviors — BL-ORD-003 is semantically about shipments, not filtering. Consider adding a dedicated BL-ORD-* entry for orders-list query behavior, or re-pointing these to a more appropriate invariant. Documented for follow-up; NOT addressed in this run.
